steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The word “that” introduces a noun clause with the subject “the rate of speciation.” This noun clause requires a verb form to connect the subject to “triple.”

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content: A noun clause must have a finite (conjugated) verb as its main verb. “Will be” is the only finite option and correctly expresses the future prediction. The other choices are non-finite forms—a participle and infinitives—that cannot function as the main verb of a noun clause. That matches choice D — D is correct.
